items.title: How is there still a ceasefire as the US blockades the Strait of Hormuz and Iran seizes ships?
items.summary: As part of its blockade of Iran, ships have been seized by US forces, others fired upon by Iran and, most recently, boarded by the regime's revolutionary guards. This is all while both sides maintain there is a ceasefire.

items.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O counter-sued by radio show broadcaster
items.summary: The company that broadcast the Kyle and Jackie O Show has launched a counter claim against the duo seeking damages for breach of contract, losses in profit and advertising revenue, and legal costs.

items.title: How the new eligibility test for NDIS participants could work
items.summary: Labor's plan to create a new "assessment tool" to determine future NDIS eligibility based on functional need could backfire without the right settings, say experts.

items.title: 'Unusual' Adelaide warm weather sees residents soak up sun at beach
items.summary: Adelaide residents have been making the most of unusually warm weather for the end of April, with crowds flocking to the beach before the school holidays finish.

items.title: Albanese poised to kill off move to increase taxes on gas giants
items.summary: The government will not increase taxes on gas giants in next month's budget, echoing the industry's argument that exporters are already paying tens of billions of dollars in taxes each year.

items.title: Grapevine berry inner necrosis virus officially detected for first time in multiple Australian states
items.summary: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

items.title: David Malouf, esteemed Australian author, poet, essayist and librettist, dies aged 92
items.summary: Erudite and urbane, David Malouf — perhaps most famous for his portrait of mid-century Brisbane in his 1975 novel Johnno — will be fondly remembered for his immeasurable contribution to Australian letters.

items.title: Canavan's campaigning in Farrer proves a frosty foray
items.summary: This week the Albanese government pushed out a major reform ahead of the federal budget, the Nationals leader was out in the cold on the campaign trail and a former Treasury secretary had some choice words about the push for gas tax reform.

items.title: James Valentine made radio look easy. He was brave and generous on air and off
items.summary: Children grew up with him, adults grew old with him, he kept us connected to our city, the world and each other. James helped us make sense of life and gave us permission to laugh at the absurdity of it all — and ourselves.

items.title: Keiron Beau James Hedges charged with threatening to 'blow up' Perth Airport terminal
items.summary: A man who allegedly threatened to "blow up" Perth Airport's main international terminal, forcing its evacuation and causing traveller chaos, has appeared in court.

items.title: Indonesia's finance minister suggests Strait of Malacca toll before immediately backtracking
items.summary: The suggestion of levying ships using the Strait of Malacca was suggested and then walked back by Indonesia's finance minister, but has prompted a response from Canberra.

items.title: Michaela Leonard to become Wallaroos' most-capped player in Anzac Day Test against New Zealand
items.summary: Rugby was not on Michaela Leonard's radar when she was a child in Canberra, but she is about to achieve a historic milestone when the Wallaroos meet the Black Ferns on Anzac Day.

items.title: State and territory ministers react to the Albanese government's NDIS changes
items.summary: State leaders are reacting to the sweeping cuts coming for the National Disability Insurance Scheme, with one minister arguing the federal government will "walk away" from those in need.

items.title: Women on mission to preserve history of El Arish's soldier settlers
items.summary: Anzac Day holds meaning for every Australian, but for Marie Carman and Lorelei King, it is another reminder of their deeply personal mission to uncover the forgotten stories of the soldiers who helped build their town.

items.title: Leading Trump envoy asks FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup amid Pope Leo XIV attacks
items.summary: As the US president feuds with Pope Leo, one of his top envoys suggests giving Italy a World Cup spot could mend fences.

items.title: Jackson Stacker inquest recommends his death be referred to homicide squad
items.summary: The NSW state coroner recommends the death of van-packer Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay be referred to the Unsolved Homicide Squad.

items.title: Authors ditch University of Queensland Press after Indigenous kids book pulled for 'anti-Semitism' of illustrator
items.summary: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past "anti-Semitic" comments.

items.title: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan sheds a tear ahead of long-awaited Gold Coast Suns debut
items.summary: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan endured a year from hell in 2025, but things are looking up for him this season as he prepares to make his Gold Coast debut.

items.title: Australia's world-leading Wedgetail surveillance planes coming up for replacement, Defence planning reveals
items.summary: Australia's fleet of world-leading surveillance aircraft is set to be replaced and analysts say the planes could be replaced by drones.

items.title: Mouse plague fears deepen as chemical regulator rejects CSIRO bait research
items.summary: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

items.title: 'Bordering on bullying': Voss hits out at coverage of Hollands
items.summary: Michael Voss has called out the media for the way it has covered Elijah Hollands's medical episode, saying the coverage of the 23-year-old "bordered on bullying".

items.title: Iranian state TV airs video of two ships being seized in the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Netflix starts filming series Breakers in Western Australia's Wheatbelt
items.summary: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

items.title: Petition against new McDonald's at Cowes, Phillip Island, receives 4,100 signatures
items.summary: The fast food giant is facing backlash after lodging a planning permit application to build a new restaurant in tourist hotspot Cowes.

items.title: James Valentine, ABC talkback radio star, dies aged 64 after cancer diagnosis
items.summary: The presenter and professional saxophone player is being remembered for his ability to explore the humour and oddities of daily life on the airwaves.

items.title: ABC's James Valentine swapped microphones — and found a new audience who loved him
items.summary: James Valentine started his career on the stage, but it was his time behind the microphone in the studio and an interactive style that came to define him.

items.title: Study shows removing exposure to plastic from our food chain yields quick results
items.summary: Perth researchers find levels of plastic chemicals in the body can be reduced within a week by avoiding plastic food packaging and kitchen utensils.

items.title: Chelsea sack Liam Rosenior after just four months, Manchester City go top of Premier League with win at relegated Burnley
items.summary: Chelsea sack Liam Rosenior after a desperate run of form saw them drop to seventh in the Premier League table, while Manchester City moved top following a 1-0 win at Burnley.

items.title: Daria Kasatkina hits world ranking low after early Madrid Open exit
items.summary: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

items.title: PM says move to next fuel security level 'not imminent' — as it happened
items.summary: Today's national cabinet meeting was "constructive", Anthony Albanese says, confirming that Australia remains at level 2 on its national fuel security plan. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

items.title: Bikie kingpin's private phone calls exposed in Mongols drug extortion case
items.summary: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

items.title: Mitch Marsh 50 not enough as Lucknow Super Giants fall to fourth straight Indian Premier League defeat
items.summary: Australia T20 skipper Mitch Marsh did his best to bail out Lucknow Super Giants, but his half-century wasn't enough to save them from defeat against Rajasthan.

items.title: SA's scandal-plagued Electoral Commission goes to ground in a crisis
items.summary: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

items.title: Disability community 'scared' by dramatic NDIS changes as government defends overhaul
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler has defended the government's latest cost-cutting changes to the scheme in an interview with the ABC, amid an avalanche of participant concern.

items.title: $7,000 NDIS cuts to social funding could reduce 'only interaction with outside world'
items.summary: An average cut of $7,000 to NDIS participant plans could hurt social supports, providing the "only interaction with the outside world" for some people with disability, advocates warn, as the minister concedes the savings will have a material impact on participant plans.

items.title: Australian musicians claim they were ripped-off by 'predatory' booking agency
items.summary: A triple j hack investigation uncovers allegations that a booking agency has failed to pay more than 20 Australian bands and artists.

items.title: Victorian government announces $365m investment in CFA, forest firefighter upgrades
items.summary: The Victorian government has unveiled its latest investment in CFA and forest firefighting upgrades.

items.title: Pacific leaders declared a fuel emergency but some didn't know about it. It's not clear what happens next
items.summary: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

items.title: More than a third of Australians seeking food relief are doing so for the first time, new survey finds
items.summary: About one in three people accessing food relief services are doing so for the first time, according to a new report from Food charity OzHarvest.

items.title: Major US firms granted access to Anthropic's Mythos to prepare against AI cyber threats, as Australian firms left out
items.summary: Local banks, power providers and infrastructure firms do not have access to test their systems against a powerful new AI cybersecurity risk, Anthropic's Mythos, which has not been made public.

items.title: Tough NDIS budget decision could prove the exception — or the template
items.summary: As far as tough budget decisions go, we haven't seen anything on this scale since Joe Hockey's ill-fated 2014 effort.

items.title: First home buyer scheme found to be fuelling price increases at lower end of market, Cotality suggests
items.summary: New research from property analytics firm Cotality appears to show that the Home Guarantee Scheme, intended to help first home buyers, may be worsening entry level affordability.

items.title: Labor's NDIS savings plan hinges on state-run supports
items.summary: Labor's plan to rein in NDIS growth will result in more than 160,000 people shifting off the scheme, with states expected to deliver alternative "foundational supports".

items.title: Fuel crisis making access to healthcare prohibitively expensive in regional areas
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Waste-to-energy plant proposed for Vuda Point in Fiji sparks fierce backlash
items.summary: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Resources companies fight gas tax push at parliamentary inquiry
items.summary: An inquiry into the taxation of gas resources has heard from proponents for a tax, who believe Australians are being robbed of their fair share of wealth from the nation's natural resources, and opponents, who argue touching tax settings would spook investors.

items.title: People with autism to be at centre of 160,000 NDIS removals
items.summary: Minister Mark Butler announces his decision to remove more than 160,000 people from the national disability scheme over the next four years, with the timing labelled "troublesome" by autism advocates.

items.title: Bombing of Darwin war veteran urges young people to learn about largest attack on Australia
items.summary: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

items.title: How will the changes to the NDIS affect me? Five key takeaways from the government's planned overhaul
items.summary: Health Minister Mark Butler has unveiled sweeping cuts to the ballooning National Disability Insurance Scheme, aimed at cutting costs of the program down to $55 billion by the end of the decade.

items.title: NSW Public Guardian caseload likely took focus away from sick client, inquest hears
items.summary: An inquest into the death of 54-year-old Raymond Wheatley heard his court-assigned guardian was juggling up to 60 other cases in the months leading up to his death.

items.title: Tasmanian salmon industry launches campaign to get 'truth' out, amid calls for shutdown
items.summary: A new advertising campaign featuring the stories of workers adjacent to Tasmania's fish farming industry is launched amid calls for the sector to be shut down.

items.title: More than 3 million Australians over 65 to pay more for private health
items.summary: The federal government argues the move will help re-establish fairness across the generations.

items.title: Netball Australia CEO Stacey West steps down as sport prepares for new era
items.summary: Stacey West will step down as CEO of Netball Australia at the end of this week, ending a two-and-a-half-year tenure that has reshaped the sport in this country.

items.title: How these households divide and conquer mealtimes
items.summary: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

items.title: Ballarat man bailed for dozens of offences including culpable driving arrested at the wheel in Canadian
items.summary: A man released on bail for about 60 charges, including two counts of culpable driving causing death, has been arrested at the wheel near Ballarat.

items.title: Body found in New Zealand after deluge causes major flooding in Wellington
items.summary: Police have found the body of a man as floodwaters recede in New Zealand's capital and the clean-up begins.

items.title: Man accused of killing Labor identity Tim Picton pleads guilty over Kalamunda assault
items.summary: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

items.title: After 27 years and 20 losses, can South Sydney finally find their first win in Melbourne?
items.summary: The Rabbitohs have never won a game in Melbourne in almost three decades of trying but they might never get a better chance than this weekend.

items.title: Millie Elliott returns, Teagan Berry to debut as NSW Sky Blues name Women's State of Origin I team
items.summary: The Sky Blues welcome back Millie Elliott for the 2025 Women's State of Origin series opener, while Emma Verran's pregnancy opens up a spot for Teagan Berry to make her debut.

items.title: Farmers fear potential mouse plague as CSIRO warns of high numbers for SA
items.summary: As if the cost of diesel and the lack of fertiliser were not enough, South Australia's farmers are also facing the prospect of a mouse plague.

items.title: Timorese remember the child heroes who helped Anzacs in World War II
items.summary: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

items.title: Canberra police hunting for man who allegedly assaulted jogger and approached child in separate incidents
items.summary: Two separate incidents nearly a year apart in Canberra’s south appeared unrelated until ACT Policing used DNA analysis to link them to the same man.
